title: euphrasia gracilis var. tetraquetra
intro: Euphrasia gracilis var. tetraquetra, also known as the mountain eye or four-angled eyebright, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating eye and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Euphrasia gracilis var. tetraquetra include its ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reduce swelling and redness, and alleviate congestion and coughs, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Euphrasia gracilis var. tetraquetra can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or eye drops, allowing for various forms of herbal application.
